tidyfault 0.0.0.9
  • Reference

    get_gate() Function

    get_gate.Rd

    This function produces the gate polygons for a set of supplied gate types and their x and y coordinates.

    get_gate(x, y, gate, size = 1, res = 50)

    Arguments

    x

    (Required) Numeric value or vector representing the x coordinate(s) of the gate center in the layout.

    y

    (Required) Numeric value or vector representing the y coordinate(s) of the gate center in the layout.

    gate

    (Required) Character vector of gate type names. Must contain one or more of "top", "and", or "or". If multiple values are provided, only the first unique value is used.

    size

    (Default = 1) Numeric value controlling the diameter of the gate polygon. The shape is scaled proportionally around the center point. Default is 1.

    res

    (Default = 50) Numeric value controlling the resolution (number of line segments) used to draw curved gate shapes. Higher values create smoother curves. Default is 50.

    Value

    A data.frame (tibble) with columns x and y containing coordinate pairs that define the gate polygon shape. The coordinates are centered at the provided (x, y) position and scaled according to size. The shape depends on the gate type: AND gates have a rounded rectangle shape, OR gates have a curved shape, and top events have a rectangular shape.

    Details

    This function is a wrapper that selects and generates the appropriate gate shape based on the gate type:

    • Determines the gate type from the gate parameter (uses first unique value if multiple provided)

    • Calls the appropriate shape-generating function: gate_and() for AND gates, gate_or() for OR gates, or gate_top() for top events

    • Scales and translates the base shape to match the specified (x, y) coordinates and size parameter

    • Returns coordinates ready for use with geom_polygon() in ggplot2

    The function handles coordinate transformation using scales::rescale() to position the gate shape correctly. Each gate type has a distinct visual representation following standard fault tree analysis conventions.

    See also

    gate_and for AND gate shape generation, gate_or for OR gate shape generation, gate_top for top event shape generation

    Contents

    Developed by Timothy Fraser, Jingyao Tong.
